item.text: The reproduction tests are present in `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/tests/error.rs`; I’m reading the rest of the error implementation now to identify the exact missing conversion site and the narrowest edit.

item.text: `error.rs` defines `CanonicalError`, accessors, `Display`, and `std::error::Error`, but there are no `From<std::io::Error>` or `From<serde_json::Error>` impls. I’m checking the local builder/context APIs so the proposal uses existing constructors instead of inventing a parallel path.

item.text: The crate already depends on `serde_json`, and `CanonicalError::internal(detail).create()` plus `InvalidArgument::format(...)`/`__invalid_argument(...)` provide the intended categories locally. I’m running the focused test once to confirm the current failure mode before submitting the artifact.

item.text: The focused test fails exactly on the four missing conversions: direct `CanonicalError::from` calls and `?` propagation both require `From<std::io::Error>` / `From<serde_json::Error>`. I’m submitting the fix proposal now with the local edit only in `error.rs`.

arguments.summary_title: Add common error conversions for CanonicalError
arguments.root_cause: `CanonicalError` defines its enum, accessors, `Display`, and `std::error::Error` in `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs`, but the impl block ends at lines 553-559 with no `impl From<std::io::Error> for CanonicalError` or `impl From<serde_json::Error> for CanonicalError`. Because Rust's `?` operator uses `From`/`Into` to convert the source error into the function's error type, `std::io::Error` and `serde_json::Error` cannot be propagated into `Result<_, CanonicalError>`, and direct `CanonicalError::from(err)` resolves only to the identity `From<CanonicalError>` impl from core.
arguments.fix_description: In `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs`, add two narrow `From` implementations near the existing `Display`/`std::error::Error` impls. Implement `From<std::io::Error> for CanonicalError` by returning `CanonicalError::internal(err.to_string()).create()`, which produces the existing Internal canonical category with status 500/title `Internal`. Implement `From<serde_json::Error> for CanonicalError` by returning `CanonicalError::__invalid_argument(InvalidArgument::format(err.to_string()))` (or an equivalent local construction that preserves the serde message as `detail`), which produces status 400/title `Invalid Argument` and makes `detail()` equal to the serde_json error message. No new dependency is needed because `serde_json` is already a dependency of `cf-modkit-canonical-errors`.
arguments.reasoning: The reproduction tests fail at compile time: direct `CanonicalError::from(std::io::Error)` and `CanonicalError::from(serde_json::Error)` report mismatched types, and `?` propagation reports that `From<std::io::Error>` and `From<serde_json::Error>` are not implemented for `CanonicalError`. The target crate already has category construction logic in the same module: `CanonicalError::internal(...).create()` maps to the Internal variant, while `InvalidArgument::format(...)` and `CanonicalError::__invalid_argument(...)` produce an InvalidArgument variant whose default detail is the format message. Adding these two impls at the error type's definition site is the smallest change that satisfies both direct conversion and `?` propagation without changing builders, public enum variants, status/title accessors, or unrelated ModKit runtime behavior.
alternative_approaches: Add public constructor helpers such as `CanonicalError::from_io_error` and `CanonicalError::from_json_error`, but that would not make `?` work because `?` requires `From`/`Into`.
Route serde errors through the public resource-error builder API, but that requires more machinery than the local `InvalidArgument::format` context and risks changing resource fields unnecessarily.
arguments.summary_markdown:
## Bug
`cf_modkit_errors::CanonicalError` cannot be created from `std::io::Error` or `serde_json::Error`, so both `CanonicalError::from(err)` and `?` propagation into `Result<_, CanonicalError>` fail to compile.

## Cause
`libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs` implements `Display` and `std::error::Error` for `CanonicalError`, but it does not implement `From<std::io::Error>` or `From<serde_json::Error>`. Rust therefore has no conversion path for direct `from` calls or for the implicit conversion used by `?`.

## Fix
Add two local `From` impls in `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs`:

- `std::io::Error` -> `CanonicalError::internal(err.to_string()).create()` for 500 / `Internal`.
- `serde_json::Error` -> invalid-argument canonical error using `InvalidArgument::format(err.to_string())`, preserving the serde message as `detail()`, for 400 / `Invalid Argument`.

No dependency changes are required; `serde_json` is already in the crate dependencies.

## Confidence
0.93 — the focused test currently fails only because the two `From` impls are missing, and the crate already has the exact category constructors needed for the expected behavior.

arguments.summary_title: Add common error conversions for CanonicalError
arguments.root_cause: `CanonicalError` defines its enum, accessors, `Display`, and `std::error::Error` in `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs`, but the impl block ends at lines 553-559 with no `impl From<std::io::Error> for CanonicalError` or `impl From<serde_json::Error> for CanonicalError`. Because Rust's `?` operator uses `From`/`Into` to convert the source error into the function's error type, `std::io::Error` and `serde_json::Error` cannot be propagated into `Result<_, CanonicalError>`, and direct `CanonicalError::from(err)` resolves only to the identity `From<CanonicalError>` impl from core.
arguments.fix_description: In `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs`, add two narrow `From` implementations near the existing `Display`/`std::error::Error` impls. Implement `From<std::io::Error> for CanonicalError` by returning `CanonicalError::internal(err.to_string()).create()`, which produces the existing Internal canonical category with status 500/title `Internal`. Implement `From<serde_json::Error> for CanonicalError` by returning `CanonicalError::__invalid_argument(InvalidArgument::format(err.to_string()))` (or an equivalent local construction that preserves the serde message as `detail`), which produces status 400/title `Invalid Argument` and makes `detail()` equal to the serde_json error message. No new dependency is needed because `serde_json` is already a dependency of `cf-modkit-canonical-errors`.
arguments.reasoning: The reproduction tests fail at compile time: direct `CanonicalError::from(std::io::Error)` and `CanonicalError::from(serde_json::Error)` report mismatched types, and `?` propagation reports that `From<std::io::Error>` and `From<serde_json::Error>` are not implemented for `CanonicalError`. The target crate already has category construction logic in the same module: `CanonicalError::internal(...).create()` maps to the Internal variant, while `InvalidArgument::format(...)` and `CanonicalError::__invalid_argument(...)` produce an InvalidArgument variant whose default detail is the format message. Adding these two impls at the error type's definition site is the smallest change that satisfies both direct conversion and `?` propagation without changing builders, public enum variants, status/title accessors, or unrelated ModKit runtime behavior.
alternative_approaches: Add public constructor helpers such as `CanonicalError::from_io_error` and `CanonicalError::from_json_error`, but that would not make `?` work because `?` requires `From`/`Into`.
Route serde errors through the public resource-error builder API, but that requires more machinery than the local `InvalidArgument::format` context and risks changing resource fields unnecessarily.
arguments.summary_markdown:
## Bug
`cf_modkit_errors::CanonicalError` cannot be created from `std::io::Error` or `serde_json::Error`, so both `CanonicalError::from(err)` and `?` propagation into `Result<_, CanonicalError>` fail to compile.

## Cause
`libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs` implements `Display` and `std::error::Error` for `CanonicalError`, but it does not implement `From<std::io::Error>` or `From<serde_json::Error>`. Rust therefore has no conversion path for direct `from` calls or for the implicit conversion used by `?`.

## Fix
Add two local `From` impls in `libs/modkit-canonical-errors/src/error.rs`:

- `std::io::Error` -> `CanonicalError::internal(err.to_string()).create()` for 500 / `Internal`.
- `serde_json::Error` -> invalid-argument canonical error using `InvalidArgument::format(err.to_string())`, preserving the serde message as `detail()`, for 400 / `Invalid Argument`.

No dependency changes are required; `serde_json` is already in the crate dependencies.

## Confidence
0.93 — the focused test currently fails only because the two `From` impls are missing, and the crate already has the exact category constructors needed for the expected behavior.
